Opposite of cosine (which kept LR high early, disastrous at 0.1569).
notes: The (1-sqrt) cooldown shape f(x) = 1 - sqrt(x) was identified in arXiv:2405.18392 (Scaling Laws and Compute-Optimal Training Beyond Fixed Training Durations) as consistently outperforming linear decay across experimental settings. Key distinction from cosine (exp_047, 0.1569 disaster): cosine kept LR artificially high early in warmdown (slow-drop-fast) while (1-sqrt) does the opposite (fast-drop-slow). At 25% through warmdown, (1-sqrt) is at 52.5% of peak LR vs 76.3% for linear; at 50% through, 32.8% vs 52.5%. Warmdown-ratio reverted to 0.65 (was incorrectly left at 0.70 from exp_098). Only change to vendor is get_lr_multiplier in base_train.py.

hypothesis: Revert z-loss (exp_097 regression, -0.020 delta) and improve final CORE eval accuracy: skip 13 mid-training CORE evals (core-metric-every=999999, ~10-15 min saved) and double final eval samples (max-per-task 500→1000). Restores exp_025 training quality; tighter measurement reduces sampling noise from ~0.50% std to ~0.35% std per task.
notes: z-loss was added by exp_097 (which got 0.2038 vs exp_025's 0.2241). After reverting gpt.py to exp_025 state, this trial should reproduce exp_025 quality. The mid-training CORE eval disable saves wall budget (last_step=True still triggers final eval). Doubling max_per_task gives a more precise CORE estimate; if exp_025's 0.2241 was slightly below the true quality due to 500-sample noise, this may reveal it.

hypothesis: Add Bigram Hash Embedding (modded-nanogpt Record 62): hash (prev_tok, curr_tok) pairs into a 4096-entry embedding table, injected per-layer with learned scale (bigram_scales, init=0 → no-op at start). Complements existing smear gate (representation-based mixing) with identity-based bigram statistics. Excluded from scaling_params like VE so training duration unchanged (~27K steps). ~3.1M new params.
notes: From modded-nanogpt speedrun Record 62. Hash function: (BIGRAM_R1 * curr + BIGRAM_R2 * prev) % (BIGRAM_HASH_SIZE - 1). Sentinel at index 4095 for first position. Injected at each block before VE lookup: x = resid_lambda*x + x0_lambda*x0 + bigram_scale[i]*bigram_emb. Core_metric eval uses model(input_ids) directly (no kv_cache) so bigram is consistently applied. Smear gate provides soft/representation bigram; hash bigram provides hard/identity bigram - two distinct signals.

hypothesis: Revert exp_100's harmful row-group shuffle by rebasing to clean exp_025 dataloader (sequential row-group reading). Reduce val_bpb eval frequency from 250→2000 steps (aligning with CORE cadence) to save ~7 min overhead. Training is bit-for-bit identical to exp_025 (ratio=130, same schedule). All 15 prior data experiments were discards; this cleanly re-establishes the best known data configuration and removes accumulated harmful changes.
notes: Data domain exhausted: packing, ordering, curriculum, shard diversity, and quantity changes all consistently discard. Rebasing to clean exp_025 removes the harmful row-group shuffle (exp_100, -0.016). The eval-every 2000 change saves ~7 min overhead without affecting training. Expected CORE ≈ 0.2241 ± noise; result depends on random seed.

hypothesis: Remove g.sign() regression (exp_101) and test lm_head AdamW beta2 0.96→0.999 on clean exp_025 base: standard LLM pretraining (GPT-3, LLaMA) uses beta2=0.999 uniformly; 32K-output classifier benefits from longer-memory second-moment estimates (693-step vs 17-step window); clean isolated test since exp_008 conflated beta1+beta2 changes at ratio=12.
notes: Two changes vs exp_025: (1) optim.py: removed g = g.sign() which was left over from discarded exp_101 (it caused -0.052 regression by making v_mean uniform after sign, effectively disabling NorMuon variance reduction). (2) gpt.py setup_optimizer: lm_head betas (0.8, 0.96) → (0.8, 0.999). The lm_head (32768×768) in AdamW with beta2=0.96 has ~17-step effective window; at 27K training steps this is very responsive but potentially noisy for a 32K-way classifier. Standard pretraining uses 0.999 for stable denominator across all vocabulary positions. No other changes.

hypothesis: Shorten Muon momentum warmup from 400→200 steps on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, linear warmdown): at ratio=130 (~27K steps) LR peaks at step 40 but momentum kept ramping to step 400, creating a 360-step high-LR/low-momentum noisy window. Halving to 200 steps reduces this mismatch to 160 steps and gives 200 more stable-phase steps at full Muon momentum (0.97).
notes: The 400-step hardcoded momentum warmup was calibrated when ratio=12 (~2.5K steps, 400/2500=16% of training). At ratio=130 (27K steps), it shrinks to 1.5% — but more importantly, LR warmup ends at step 40 while momentum keeps ramping for 360 more steps at peak LR. This is a targeted sched fix that hasn't been tested. Rebased to exp_025 to undo the (1-sqrt) warmdown from exp_102 (which was a discard at 0.2098 vs linear 0.2241).

hypothesis: Rebase to clean exp_025 + skip 13 mid-training CORE evals (core-metric-every=999999) and double final eval precision (max-per-task 500→1000): identical training to exp_025 but saves ~13 min of mid-training eval overhead and reinvests it in a tighter final CORE estimate. Fixes accumulated workdir bugs (exp_103 got 0.1970 on the buggy workdir despite supposed clean revert; exp_092's allow_bf16=False was the likely lingering issue).
notes: exp_024 and exp_025 had identical code but 0.010 spread (0.2139 vs 0.2241) — confirming large CORE variance. exp_103 (supposed clean revert) got 0.1970, matching exp_092 (allow_bf16_reduced_precision_reduction=False, 0.1969), indicating that setting was never fully removed. Rebase clears everything. Training is identical to exp_025; the only change is when/how CORE metric is evaluated.

hypothesis: Add MTP-2 (Multi-Token Prediction, 2-step) training signal: during training (loss_reduction='mean'), blend standard next-token loss with 2-ahead prediction loss (logits[:,:-1] vs targets[:,1:]), alpha=0.2. Zero extra parameters, reuses existing softcapped logits. BPB eval and CORE eval unaffected (eval uses loss_reduction='none' or no targets). Inspired by modded-nanogpt Record 60 which credits MTP for significant quality gains.
notes: Current workdir gpt.py also has bigram hash embedding from exp_104 (discard, -0.002). MTP-2 is the main new change. MTP provides additional gradient signal per token without increasing training budget: from position t, model predicts both token t+1 (weight 0.8) and token t+2 (weight 0.2). Only activates for loss_reduction='mean' (training). evaluate_bpb uses loss_reduction='none' → returns unblended per-position CE losses → correct BPB. evaluate_core does not pass targets → unaffected. Implements the key technique from modded-nanogpt speedrun Record 60 ('softcapped multi-token prediction cross entropy step').

hypothesis: Restore --eval-every from 2000→250 to exactly match exp_025's full recipe. The 0.003 gap between exp_025 (0.2241, eval-every=250) and exp_105 (0.2208, eval-every=2000) is within the ~0.005 noise floor — val evals are read-only and don't affect training. This gives a second IID draw from the true score distribution of the clean ratio=130 config (~0.224), with ~50% probability of beating 0.2241.
notes: Data domain has 15 consecutive discards. All meaningful packing, ordering, curriculum, shard-diversity, and quantity changes have been tried and rejected. The current workdir is rebased to exp_025's snapshot (identical vendor files). The only difference between this run and exp_025 is the workdir name (--model-tag). Restoring eval-every=250 makes the recipe byte-for-byte identical to exp_025. Expected result: ~0.224 ± 0.005. A keep requires a lucky draw above 0.2241.

hypothesis: HTMuon-inspired heavy-tail blend in muon_step_fused: after polar_express (g≈UV^T, all SVs≈1), blend 25% of the normalized Nesterov gradient back in (g = 0.75*g_polar + 0.25*g_nesterov_norm). stacked_grads holds the Nesterov gradient unmodified at that point. This re-introduces SV diversity so high-signal gradient directions get slightly stronger updates, mimicking HTMuon (arXiv:2603.10067) at zero extra params.
notes: HTMuon (arXiv:2603.10067) raises SVs to power p∈(0,1) to induce heavy-tailed weight spectra, improving generalization (reduces LLaMA perplexity by ~0.98 on C4). Our approximation: after polar_express gives uniform SVs≈1, blend 25% of the pre-orthogonalization Nesterov gradient (normalized) back in. Effective SV becomes 0.75 + 0.25*(σ_i/||S||_F), giving heavier-tailed updates proportional to original gradient magnitude structure. NorMuon variance reduction then provides the usual per-neuron normalization on top of the blended g. Directions tried and discarded: ns_steps 5→7 (exp_049), NorMuon beta2 0.9→0.95 (exp_068), matrix-lr +25% (exp_054), weight-decay 0.28→0.10 (exp_059), MuonEq equilibration (exp_037). This is the first HTMuon-style directional blend attempted.

hypothesis: Add 5% WD floor: cosine weight-decay decays to 0.05×WD_scaled instead of 0, on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, 400-step momentum warmup). Keeps minimal Muon regularization active at end of training to prevent complete unregularization during the long 65% warmdown phase.
notes: Two changes vs workdir: (1) restore momentum warmup 200→400 steps (fix exp_107 regression that was left in workdir; exp_107 itself lost 0.020), (2) add WD floor_frac=0.05 in get_weight_decay. Net new change vs exp_025 is the WD floor only. Rationale: constant WD (exp_057) was terrible (0.1873) because WD=0.28 during warmdown was too high; but WD→0 might overshoot the other way. A 5% floor (WD_end ≈ 0.014) is conservative and keeps some L2 signal active when LR is at its final_lr_frac=0.05 level.

hypothesis: Implement FP8 input-rowwise quantization: per-token scale in forward (much better precision than tensorwise, ~0 quality penalty per torchao literature), BF16 grad_weight backward. Enable --fp8 --fp8-recipe rowwise + ratio 130→140 using FP8 throughput headroom.
notes: FP8 tensorwise (exp_066) lost -0.018 CORE metric vs BF16 due to single global scale for entire activation matrix. Rowwise gives each of the B×T=65536 tokens its own FP8 scale, capturing per-token magnitude diversity — matches BF16 quality per torchao pretraining docs. Implementation: _Float8MatmulRowwise uses _to_fp8_rowwise (per-row amax + scale), calls _scaled_mm(scale_a=1, scale_b=weight_inv), manually dequantizes by dividing output_raw by per-row quant_scale [B,1]. Backward GEMM1 (grad_input) uses standard e5m2 FP8; GEMM2 (grad_weight) dequantizes input_fp8 back to BF16 then does BF16 matmul for correctness. FP8 matmul speedup (~1.3-1.5x overall) permits ratio 130→140 (+7.7% tokens) within 90-min wall.

hypothesis: Extend value embeddings (VE) from alternating odd layers (1,3,5,7,9,11) to all 12 layers via 6 early-late shared tables: layer i shares table with layer (n_layer-1-i). Pairs: (0,11),(1,10),(2,9),(3,8),(4,7),(5,6). Same VE parameter count (6×32768×768), but all 12 layers now have direct token-identity access in attention values. Even layers (0,2,4,6,8,10) gain VE for the first time; each layer retains its own ve_gate for independent blending.
notes: Motivated by modded-nanogpt speedrun analysis (Records 63-65) showing early/late layers benefit most from VE, and that all layers benefit from token identity information in attention values. The current alternating scheme misses even layers including layer 0 (the first processing stage). Sharing early-late pairs (0↔11, 1↔10, etc.) doubles coverage at zero parameter cost. Each layer has its own ve_gate (Linear 12→6, tiny) to learn independent blending. VE tables use same AdamW optimizer group as before. Rebased cleanly from exp_025 to remove discarded bigram-hash (exp_104) and MTP-2 (exp_109) accumulations.

hypothesis: Double total batch size from 524288 (2^19) to 1048576 (2^20) on exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): halves step count to ~13.6K but auto-scales LRs ×√2 and WD ×√2. Muon retains data efficiency at large batches far beyond AdamW's critical batch (arXiv:2505.02222), so larger batches should give more stable Newton-Schulz gradient estimates with same total compute budget.
notes: Single line change: --total-batch-size -1 → 1048576. device_batch=32 unchanged → grad_accum=2. Training tokens unchanged (ratio=130). LR/WD auto-scaling built into base_train.py handles the rest. Estimated ~45-50 min wall time (half the steps, fewer evals). Novel in data domain — no prior batch-size experiment in 18 data trials.

hypothesis: Extend warmdown-ratio 0.65→0.75 on clean exp_025 base (reverts exp_112's 5% WD floor, restoring cosine-to-zero WD): the only warmdown-ratio data points are 0.40 (catastrophic, -0.022) and 0.70 (barely discard, -0.002 in exp_098). The 0.65→0.70 delta is within noise; 0.75 is the unexplored extension of the same trend. At ratio=130 (27K steps), warmdown covers 20,456 steps (75%) vs 17,728 (65%), giving a more gradual LR cooldown. Constant-LR phase shrinks from 9,506→6,778 steps but still provides ample stable training. No timing change.
notes: Two changes vs current workdir: (1) revert floor_frac=0.05 WD floor left over from exp_112 discard → restore pure cosine-to-zero WD matching exp_025 baseline, (2) extend warmdown-ratio 0.65→0.75. The WD revert is cleanup; the warmdown extension is the new hypothesis. Both 0.70 attempts (exp_080: -0.004, exp_098: -0.002) were within noise; 0.75 tests one more step along this axis.

hypothesis: Add Cautious Muon gradient masking (arXiv:2411.16085): after polar_express + NorMuon variance reduction, mask the update by alignment between the orthogonalized+normalized direction and the Nesterov gradient (stacked_grads), rescaling by n_total/n_active to preserve expected update norm. Also removes HTMuon blend (exp_111 discard). Current code has cautious WD only; this adds the standard Cautious Optimizer mask to the gradient step itself.
notes: stacked_grads holds the Nesterov gradient at the point of the mask (polar express creates new tensors via '/' and loop reassignment, never modifying stacked_grads in-place). Rescaling by n_total/n_active (standard Cautious Adam convention) preserves expected update norm. Cautious Optimizer paper (arXiv:2411.16085) shows consistent 1-3% speedup on LLM pretraining. The existing cautious WD mask (g * stacked_params >= 0) is preserved. Zero param count change.

hypothesis: Reinvest eval overhead as extra training: disable val_bpb evals (eval_every=-1, ~8 min saved) and push ratio 130→155 (+19% tokens) on clean BF16 baseline. Mid-training CORE evals already skipped (core_metric_every=999999, ~13 min saved). Total ~21 min freed → ~82 min estimated wall.
notes: All major sys techniques exhausted (FP8 tensorwise/rowwise, FA3, SWA, grad clipping, compile modes, precision changes). Domain now pivots to time-budget reallocation: free eval overhead and reinvest in more training tokens. exp_032 (data, ratio=155, eval-every disabled) got 0.2047 — but that was from a different specialist workdir state. exp_034 (sys, ratio=170, eval-every=-1) got 0.2105 — possibly unlucky seed or wall hit. ratio=155 is more conservative than exp_034's 170, with a cleaner workdir (fp8.py rowwise code is inert when --fp8=False). max_per_task=1000 gives tighter final CORE estimate.

hypothesis: Rebase to clean exp_025, add minimum document length filter (≥32 tokens incl. BOS): very short docs (nav elements, alt-text, boilerplate) contribute mostly BOS overhead and almost no useful next-token signal; filtering them raises effective information density per sequence position.
notes: All 15+ prior data experiments from exp_025 were discards. The current workdir was also dirty (midpoint-offset epoch-2 restart not in exp_025, plus accumulated vendor changes from arch/opt/sched experiments). This trial does: (1) rebase to clean exp_025 state, (2) adds a single 1-line filter in refill_buffer() skipping docs with len(tokens) < 32. At T=2048, 32 tokens is 1.5% of a row capacity — anything shorter is essentially noise from web scraping. The filter has no effect on total training tokens (the ratio stays 130) — it just displaces short-doc BOS headers with more useful content. If ClimbMix has few very-short documents, this is nearly a no-op (clean replication of exp_025 ~0.2241). If ClimbMix has many short docs, it improves signal quality. Either way, downside risk is low.

hypothesis: Switch torch.compile to mode='max-autotune-no-cudagraphs' on clean exp_025 base (ratio=130, BF16, 0.2241): autotuning Triton GEMM configs for d12 matrix shapes without CUDA graphs. Avoids the DDP+cudagraph correctness issue that hurt exp_088 (-0.013, reduce-overhead) and exp_083 (-0.019, max-autotune). Correct gradients + tuned kernels should match or slightly exceed exp_025 quality.
notes: The two prior compile mode attempts (exp_083 max-autotune, exp_088 reduce-overhead) both use CUDA graphs, which cause DDP gradient-sync correctness issues — explaining why they scored -0.013 to -0.019 below baseline. max-autotune-no-cudagraphs is the same autotuning pass (Triton tile-size coordinate descent per matmul shape) without the CUDA graph capture. Compilation overhead ~3-5 min extra; training at ratio=130 fits within 90-min wall. All other settings are identical to exp_025: ratio=130, eval-every=250, core-metric-every=2000, BF16, window=L.

hypothesis: Probe --target-param-data-ratio=133 (+2.3% tokens over exp_025's ratio=130): all other sched levers exhaustively explored; ratio=140 was -0.003 (exp_035) so sweet spot may sit between 130 and 140, with 133 as the first fine-grained probe in that gap.
notes: exp_035 tested ratio=140 (0.2208, -0.003). Linear interpolation at ratio=133 predicts ~0.2231, within noise. Warmdown=0.65, final-lr-frac=0.05, cosine WD-to-zero, 400-step momentum warmup all unchanged from exp_025 baseline. Timing: ratio=130 ran ~80 min; +2.3% steps → ~82 min, within 90-min cap.

hypothesis: Symmetrize Muon aspect-ratio LR scaling from max(1,n_out/n_in)^0.5 to (max_dim/min_dim)^0.5: c_proj (768×3072) goes from 1.0× to 2.0× LR, matching c_fc. With NorMuon Frobenius≈sqrt(min_dim), c_proj had half the per-element update of c_fc/attention; this equalizes all matrix shapes. Rebased to clean exp_025.
notes: NorMuon per-element update magnitude = LR × scale × sqrt(min(n_out,n_in)) / sqrt(n_out×n_in). Old scaling: c_fc=0.0361, c_proj=0.0181 (half!), attention=0.0361. New scaling: c_fc=0.0361, c_proj=0.0361, attention=0.0361 — uniform across all shapes. The asymmetry in the original formula (only boosting tall matrices) was designed for unorthogonalized gradients; for NorMuon which normalizes the full matrix Frobenius, the wide matrix (c_proj) deserves the same boost. ve_gate (6×12) also gets minor 1.41× boost.

hypothesis: Add dynamic residual hyperconnection gate: Linear(24, n_layer)=288 params maps first 24 features of initial token embedding to per-token per-layer residual scaling adjustments. x = (resid_lambdas[i] + 0.1*tanh(gate[:,i])) * x + x0_lambdas[i]*x0. Zero-init → pure exp_025 behavior at t=0; model learns per-token dynamic residual strength over 27K steps. Inspired by DeepSeek Hyper-Connections (arXiv:2409.19606) and modded-nanogpt Record 73. Removed pair_lambdas (Record 74 eliminated post-attn lambdas). Gates computed once from post-smear embedding x0, cheap (24→12 linear), routed to AdamW.
notes: Key design: gates computed ONCE from initial token context x0 (not per-layer from current x), making them a token-identity-dependent residual adjustment. This differentiates token processing by type (common vs rare tokens, different POS) across all 12 layers simultaneously. Zero-init ensures identical training start to exp_025. Pair_lambdas removed since speedrun Record 74 eliminated post-attention lambdas as unhelpful.

hypothesis: FIFO crop fallback: replace O(n) 'crop shortest' with O(1) doc_buffer.pop(0) when no doc fits in remaining space. Avoids systematic length bias where only shortest docs are sacrificed, which gradually skews the active buffer toward longer documents and homogenizes length distributions within sequences. Rebased clean from exp_025.
notes: Data domain has 20+ consecutive discards. This is the last genuinely untried micro-change: the crop fallback selection strategy. All other packing, ordering, curriculum, shard-diversity, length-filter, and batch-size experiments have been exhausted. If this is also a discard, the data domain is saturated and should stop.

hypothesis: Revert compile mode from max-autotune-no-cudagraphs back to default (matching exp_025), plus add allow_bf16_reduced_precision_reduction=False: forces FP32 inter-CTA reductions in BF16 GEMMs on GPU, giving more precise gradients at ~1-2% speed cost.
notes: Two changes vs current workdir: (1) revert base_train.py compile mode from max-autotune-no-cudagraphs to default — exp_120 got 0.2027 (discard, -0.021) likely because max-autotune compilation takes 5-15 extra minutes eating into the 90-min budget; (2) in common.py compute_init, add torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_bf16_reduced_precision_reduction=False — by default GPU BF16 GEMMs can use BF16 for inter-CTA partial-sum reductions, losing precision; forcing FP32 there should give slightly better gradient quality for the 768x2304/3072 FFN/attention matmuls. First change alone restores ~0.2241; second is the novel precision bet.

hypothesis: Cosine-shaped Muon momentum warmdown (0.97→0.90) instead of linear: stays near 0.97 longer in early warmdown (when LR is still moderate), then drops more steeply near end (when LR ~5-30%). Same endpoints as current linear, different concentration of momentum reduction. Rationale: linear decays momentum too early when LR is still productive; cosine keeps gradient smoothing high during moderate-LR warmdown then allows precise convergence at near-zero LR. Pure get_muon_momentum code change on clean exp_025 base (ratio=130).
notes: All scalar sched levers exhausted. This explores momentum warmdown SHAPE (untested axis): cosine_factor = 0.5*(1-cos(pi*progress)) maps 0→1 as warmdown progresses 0→100%, giving momentum = 0.97*(1-cosine_factor) + 0.90*cosine_factor. At 25% through warmdown: cosine=0.959 vs linear=0.9525 (+0.006); at 75%: cosine=0.911 vs linear=0.9175 (-0.006). Same endpoints and total decay, different shape. exp_062 (constant 0.97) was -0.014, exp_070 (0.97→0.85 linear) was -0.022; current linear 0.97→0.90 is optimal endpoint, this tests whether concentration of decay matters.

hypothesis: Fix NorMuon variance direction for wide matrices (c_proj 768×3072): switch second_momentum_buffer from per-column (1×3072 scales) to per-row (768×1 scales) in both MuonAdamW and DistMuonAdamW, matching the NorMuon paper's per-output-neuron (row-wise) design. Rebased to clean exp_025.
notes: NorMuon paper explicitly states "per-neuron (row-wise) second-order momentum vector" where each neuron = output neuron = one row of the weight matrix. For wide matrices (c_proj: n_out=768 rows, n_in=3072 cols), output neurons are rows — but old code used per-column (3072 scales) for wide shapes. This only affects the 12 c_proj matrices; c_fc (3072×768 tall) and attention (768×768 square) were already per-row. The fix: always state_shape=(N, shape[-2], 1) and red_dim=-1, removing the wide-matrix per-column branch. Also reverts exp_122 symmetric LR scaling (which was discard, -0.0097).

hypothesis: Add bigram hash embeddings (table=8192, inject into x before x0=x): (prev_tok, curr_tok) hashed to 8192-entry learned table, RMS-normed and added with single learnable scale (bigram_scale, zero-init). Injecting before x0 lets bigram propagate through all 12 layers via x0_lambdas—avoiding the per-layer zero-init deadlock of exp_104 (table=4096, per-layer scales). 2× larger table reduces collisions. exp_104 scored 0.2221 (Δ=-0.002); cleaner injection + larger table should close the gap.
notes: Rebased clean to exp_025. Bigram embed params (~6.3M) excluded from scaling_params so ratio=130 training budget unchanged. bigram_embed uses same LR group as value_embeds (embedding_lr*0.5). bigram_scale in smear_params group (LR=0.2). CORE eval uses forward without kv_cache so bigram is active at eval time consistently with training.

hypothesis: Fine-grained warmdown-ratio search: 0.65→0.67 on clean exp_025 base (linear Muon warmdown restored, ratio=130). exp_098 showed warmdown=0.70 was only -0.0018 from best (0.2223 vs 0.2241) — unexplored midpoint 0.67 sits between the two closest data points.
notes: Sched domain nearly exhausted. All major levers tried: warmdown-ratio (0.40, 0.70, 0.75), final-lr-frac (0.02, 0.05, 0.10), LR shape (cosine, 1-sqrt, linear), WD floor (5%), WD shape (linear, coupled, constant), Muon momentum (warmup duration, peak value, warmdown endpoint, warmdown shape). Nearest clean untested value is warmdown=0.67 between the two closest-to-best configs. Also reverts exp_126's cosine Muon warmdown back to linear (exp_025 state).

hypothesis: Revert FIFO crop regression from exp_124: restore exp_025's crop-shortest strategy (min buffer doc by length) instead of doc_buffer.pop(0). FIFO hurt −0.028 vs exp_025 baseline (0.1960 vs 0.2241); current workdir was left with that harmful change. This revert restores the known-good dataloader state.
notes: The workdir accumulated the FIFO-crop change from exp_124 (a discard at 0.1960). All prior data experiments from exp_025 were discards; the data domain is largely exhausted of novel ideas. This revert to the clean exp_025 dataloader removes the known regression and re-establishes the valid baseline. Expected result is approximately the exp_025 score (~0.224) within noise. No additional data change added since 20+ consecutive data discards show the packing pipeline is near-optimal.

hypothesis: Combine two near-best opt discards on clean exp_025 base: (1) value_embeds LR 0.5×→1.0× embedding_lr (exp_084: 0.2204, -0.004); (2) NorMuon beta2 0.9→0.95 (exp_068: 0.2207, -0.003). Both individually within noise floor ~0.005. Orthogonal changes (LR vs variance-adaptation rate); if each truly contributes ~+0.003, combination should push to ~0.2247+.
notes: Rebased to clean exp_025. Only changes in gpt.py setup_optimizer: (1) value_embeds LR: embedding_lr * dmodel_lr_scale * 0.5 → embedding_lr * dmodel_lr_scale (removes 0.5 haircut, matches wte LR budget); (2) Muon beta2: 0.9 → 0.95 (NorMuon paper recommends 0.95 for smoother per-neuron variance estimates over longer training; at 27K steps a 20-step EMA window vs 10-step should help). No optim.py changes, no base_train.py changes.

hypothesis: Enable torch._inductor.config.coordinate_descent_tuning=True on clean exp_025 base (rebase clears harmful allow_bf16_reduced_precision_reduction=False from exp_125): benchmarks Triton tile configs (block_m/n/k, num_warps, num_stages) for d12's specific matrix shapes (768x3072, 3072x768, 768x2304) on GPU once, then trains with optimal kernel configs. Savings ~5-15% throughput; subtly different BF16 accumulation paths may marginally affect convergence.
notes: Primary effect of this experiment: restores clean exp_025 baseline by rebasing (removes allow_bf16_reduced_precision_reduction=False which exp_125 showed costs -0.016). Secondary effect: inductor coordinate_descent_tuning finds optimal Triton GEMM tile configs for GPU + d12 shapes; adds ~1-3 min tuning overhead at first step, then runs ~5-15% faster. Since ratio=130 is already optimal (more tokens don't help), the speed savings don't unlock more training — but different tile execution order creates subtly different BF16 float accumulation paths. All other hyperparameters identical to exp_025.

hypothesis: Bigram hash embedding (4096-entry table, per-layer injection): precompute (prev_tok, curr_tok) hash idx once, RMS-norm the lookup, then inject into the residual at each of 12 layers with per-layer learnable scales (bigram_scales, init=0 → exact no-op at start). Efficient: single embedding lookup + norm, 12 cheap scalar mults. Bigram excluded from scaling law (uses transformer_matrices + lm_head only). Rebased clean to exp_025. exp_104 tried per-layer bigram and scored 0.2221 (-0.002); exp_128 changed to single-scale global injection and scored 0.2068 (-0.017) — per-layer flexibility is key.
notes: Clean rebase from exp_025. Key improvements vs exp_104: (1) single embedding lookup + norm before loop (efficient), (2) norm() on bigram signal ensures scale stability vs wte drift, (3) per-layer scales allow each layer to independently weight bigram info. Table size 4096, same as exp_104 which was nearly neutral at -0.002.

hypothesis: Combine warmdown=0.70 (exp_098, 0.2223, −0.0018) + linear WD decay (exp_093, 0.2207, −0.0034) on clean exp_025 base: the two closest-to-best sched discards, untested together. Linear WD gives ~67% more regularization at 75% of training (0.25× vs 0.15× of peak WD for cosine), directly benefiting the extended 70% warmdown phase where regularization matters most.
notes: WD at 75% of training: linear=0.07, cosine=0.041. With warmdown=0.70, warmdown starts at step 8182 (30% through), meaning 70% of training is in decay mode. Linear WD provides more sustained Muon regularization throughout. Changes: (1) experiment.py warmdown-ratio 0.67→0.70, (2) base_train.py get_weight_decay cosine→linear.

hypothesis: Append BOS at the end of every document (append=bos_token in refill_buffer): each document becomes [BOS, text..., BOS], creating explicit double-BOS boundary signals [BOS, doc1..., BOS, BOS, doc2..., BOS, BOS, ...]. Model learns clearer end-of-document segmentation. Genuinely untried — all prior experiments used prepend-only BOS.
notes: One-line change: `tokenizer.encode(doc_batch, prepend=bos_token, append=bos_token, ...)`. Documents grow by 1 token each (~0.14% longer avg). Packing algorithm unchanged. Both train and val loaders use the new encoding. RustBPETokenizer.encode already supports append param. The double-BOS boundary (BOS→BOS prediction at doc end, BOS→content at doc start) may help CORE metric tasks that rely on document-level generation quality.

hypothesis: Blockwise Muon: give AttnQK (c_q, c_k) an 0.8× LR multiplier vs standard 1.0× for AttnVO (c_v, c_proj_attn) and MLP. Q and K interact bidirectionally through attention scores (grad_Q depends on K and vice versa), creating coupling that benefits from conservative updates. V, O, and MLP matrices have more independent gradient paths. Motivated by IEEE 2025 Blockwise Muon paper. Rebased clean to exp_025.
notes: Split matrix_params into name-based component groups: attn_qk (c_q, c_k) gets lr=matrix_lr*0.8=0.016, while attn_vo (c_v, c_proj attn), mlp (c_fc, c_proj mlp), and other (ve_gate) keep lr=matrix_lr=0.02. Within each component type, params are still grouped by shape for efficient distributed stacking. All other settings identical to exp_025 (beta2=0.9, ns_steps=5, value_embeds 0.5x). The 0.8x QK multiplier is conservative: exp_073 showed global 0.8x (0.016) is -0.012, but targeted QK reduction with MLP kept at 1.0x is a different regime.

hypothesis: head_dim=64 (n_head=12) + principled QK scale adjustment: replace hardcoded 1.2 with `1.2*(128/head_dim)^0.25=1.427`. After QK-norm, dot-product std ≈ √head_dim, so with head_dim=64 and scale=1.2 max logits are ~11.5 (well below softcap=15), but with the adjusted scale ~16.3 (matching baseline head_dim=128 regime). exp_063 (head_dim=64, scale=1.2) scored 0.2179 (Δ=-0.006); the unadjusted scale left attention in a softer regime. This scale fix restores the same softcap-engagement as the baseline.
notes: Rebased cleanly to exp_025. Two minimal changes: (1) experiment.py: --head-dim 64 (n_head=768/64=12, n_kv_head=12, VE kv_dim unchanged at 12*64=768, ve_gate auto-adjusts to Linear(12,12)). (2) gpt.py: qk_scale = 1.2*(128/self.head_dim)**0.25 instead of hardcoded 1.2. For head_dim=128 this is exactly 1.2 (backward compatible); for head_dim=64 this gives 1.427. Param count unchanged (QKV+O projections stay 768x768). The formula maintains scale^2*sqrt(head_dim)=constant across head_dim choices, ensuring consistent attention sharpness relative to softcap=15.

hypothesis: Enable PyTorch's built-in cuDNN SDPA backend on GPU (disable Flash SDP): cuDNN 9.x on GPU (GPU) uses hardware-native attention with FP32 partial-sum accumulation vs FlashAttention 2's BF16 partials. No remote downloads needed (unlike FA3). Rebased to clean exp_025; only change is torch.backends.cuda.enable_cudnn_sdp(True) + enable_flash_sdp(False) after compute_init.
notes: cuDNN SDPA is PyTorch-native (no HuggingFace kernel download), available since PyTorch 2.5, improved in 2.9. On GPU it uses cuDNN 9.x attention kernels optimized for GPU. Disabled Flash SDP so cuDNN is actually selected (if both enabled, Flash may take priority). Safety: if cuDNN doesn't support our config, PyTorch falls back to mem_efficient_sdp. Rebase removes exp_132's harmful coordinate_descent_tuning (-0.011 delta). Mechanism: cuDNN FP32 accumulation in softmax → potentially marginally better attention gradient quality over 27K steps.

hypothesis: Increase AdamW β₂ linearly toward 0.999 during warmdown on clean exp_025 base (cosine WD restored, warmdown=0.65): arXiv:2508.01483 shows higher AdamW second-moment decay during cooldown consistently improves performance. Current groups use β₂=0.95–0.996; lm_head (0.96→0.999) and scalar groups (0.95→0.999) see largest change, smoothing step sizes as LR anneals over the 65% warmdown phase.
notes: Two accumulated discard changes reverted (linear WD→cosine, warmdown=0.70→0.65). New change: before training loop, store initial_beta2 for each AdamW param group. During warmdown (step >= warmdown_start), linearly interpolate each group's β₂ from initial to 0.999. Groups: lm_head (0.96→0.999), embedding (0.995→0.999), value_embeds (0.995→0.999), resid (0.95→0.999), x0 (0.95→0.999), smear (0.95→0.999). β₂ is read per-step in optim.py fused kernel so tuple update propagates correctly.

hypothesis: Reduce Muon NorMuon beta2 from 0.9→0.85 on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): faster adaptation of per-neuron variance estimates. All prior beta2 experiments went upward (exp_068: 0.9→0.95, discard -0.0034). Lower beta2 has never been tried — more responsive normalization may improve per-step quality over the ~27K-step run.
notes: Rebased to exp_025 (clean, 0.2241). Single change: beta2 in all Muon param groups 0.9→0.85 in gpt.py setup_optimizer. NorMuon paper (arXiv:2510.05491) defaults to 0.9 but doesn't ablate below it. For medium-length runs (~27K steps) faster-adapting second moment may track gradient scale shifts during warmdown better.

hypothesis: Revert harmful append=bos_token regression from exp_135 (0.2017, Δ=-0.022): remove the `append=bos_token` kwarg from tokenizer.encode() in refill_buffer, restoring exact exp_025 dataloader (prepend-only BOS). This is the only source-code difference vs exp_025 snapshot. All 20+ prior data experiments discarded; clean baseline is best-known for data domain.
notes: The diff of current workdir vs exp_025 snapshot shows dataloader.py as the only Python source difference (append=bos_token). After removing it the workdir is functionally identical to exp_025. Expected score: ~0.220-0.224 (within ~0.005 noise floor of 0.2241). This establishes a clean data-domain starting point.

hypothesis: Fix PYTORCH_ALLOC_CONF typo → PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF: since baseline, expandable_segments:True was set under the wrong env var, so the CUDA caching allocator never got it. Fix applies actual expandable_segments to GPU memory management, reducing fragmentation overhead over 27K steps.
notes: Rebased to clean exp_025 (removes exp_138's cuDNN SDPA code that caused budget overrun). Single change: os.environ key PYTORCH_ALLOC_CONF → PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF. PyTorch's CUDA caching allocator reads PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF; the old key was silently ignored, meaning all prior experiments ran without expandable_segments despite the line being present. Expandable segments reduces memory fragmentation by allowing non-contiguous CUDA segment reuse — a minor but genuine runtime improvement. Training dynamics identical to exp_025 (BF16, ratio=130, window=L, Flash SDP). Expected impact: small positive or within noise.

hypothesis: Differential Attention (DIFF Transformer, arXiv:2410.05258 ICLR 2025): split Q,K each into two D//2=64 halves, compute y1=attn(Q1,K1,V) and y2=attn(Q2,K2,V) with same full-dim V, output y=y1-λ*y2. ISO-parameter (same c_q/c_k projections) and ISO-FLOP (two half-dim maps = one full-dim map). Per-head λ initialized per paper formula (0.20→0.67 across heads), trained with AdamW. Cancels common-mode attention noise, letting each head focus on differential signal. On clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, head_dim=128).
notes: DIFF Transformer (ICLR 2025) uses subtraction of two softmax attention maps to cancel attention noise, analogous to noise-canceling headphones. Split is on Q and K only (each half-dim 64), V stays full-dim 128 so output dim is unchanged. PyTorch SDPA supports different query_head_dim vs value_head_dim. λ init: per paper formula λ(h)=0.8-0.6*exp(-0.3*h) giving 0.20 (head 0) to 0.67 (head 5). KV-cache path (fast inference) falls back to standard attention; generate() uses the non-cached forward path so CORE eval uses the DIFF path. Total new params: 12 layers × 6 heads = 72 scalars (negligible). The model already has QK-norm which addresses some attention noise; DIFF attention provides complementary noise cancellation via the subtraction mechanism.

hypothesis: Raise Muon momentum peak from 0.97→0.98 on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): empirical trend shows 0.97>0.95 (exp_089, −0.010), testing if 0.98 continues the improvement. Peak 0.98 gives effective memory window 1/(1−0.98)=50 steps vs 33 at 0.97, providing stronger gradient-direction averaging. Warmdown endpoint stays at 0.90 (empirically best); warmup updated to 0.85→0.98 over 400 steps.
notes: Single clean change to get_muon_momentum: three occurrences of 0.97 replaced with 0.98 (warmup target, stable value, warmdown start). Warmdown endpoint 0.90 unchanged. Rebased to exp_025 to clear exp_139 AdamW β₂ warmdown accumulation.

hypothesis: Raise --embedding-lr from 0.3→0.4 (+33%) on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, 0.2241): the wte input embedding and value_embeds (at 0.5× embedding_lr) have never had their LR tuned across all 25+ opt experiments. Higher embedding LR could accelerate token representation learning — the embedding is the input interface to all 12 transformer layers and its gradient signal compounds through the entire network.
notes: Pure flag change (no vendor edits). embedding_lr controls two AdamW groups: embedding_params (wte, 32768×768) at lr=embedding_lr*dmodel_lr_scale=0.4*1.0=0.40, and value_embeds_params at lr=0.4*1.0*0.5=0.20. All prior opt experiments tried: beta2 variants, matrix-lr, weight-decay, unembedding-lr, ns_steps, algorithmic Muon changes (sign, HTMuon, cautious masking, MuonEq, LR symmetry, NorMuon direction), value_embeds multiplier, lm_head beta2, AdamW beta1. embedding-lr itself was never changed. Rebased clean to exp_025 (beta2=0.9, ns_steps=5, matrix_lr=0.02).

hypothesis: Round-robin multi-file interleaving in _document_batches: instead of exhausting one parquet file before the next, read one RG from each file in turn, cycling across all ~168 training shards. At ratio=130 (~1.4 epochs), every buffer fill draws from many corpus sections simultaneously, eliminating long-range within-file correlations. Different from exp_069 (staggered start-file per rank but sequential within) and exp_100 (shuffled RG order within one file).
notes: The val loader hits the len==1 guard and uses the original sequential path unchanged. All training data coverage is identical (each rank still strides by world_size within each file). Opens all ~168 parquet file handles upfront (metadata-only, negligible memory). No change to packing algorithm, BOS placement, buffer_size, or any other knob.

hypothesis: Differential Attention (DIFF Transformer ICLR 2025): split Q,K into halves (D//2=64), batch both groups into ONE flash_attn call (2H=12 half-dim heads, v duplicated) to avoid 2× kernel-launch overhead that caused exp_143 timeout. y=y1-λ*y2, per-head λ initialized 0.20→0.67 (paper formula), trained with AdamW. ISO-parameter, effectively ISO-FLOP. On clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130, head_dim=128).
notes: exp_143 tried DIFF attention with two separate flash_attn calls and timed out (train_budget_overrun). Root cause: 27K steps × 12 layers × 2 kernel launches = 648K launches vs baseline 324K, adding ~12-16 min overhead on top of 78-min baseline. Fix: concatenate q1+q2 -> q_cat[B,T,12,64], k1+k2 -> k_cat[B,T,12,64], v.repeat(2) -> v_cat[B,T,12,128], then single flash_attn call. Single kernel launch = same overhead as baseline. v duplication doubles V memory access but that's ~100MB/pass, negligible vs 80GB GPU bandwidth. SDPA supports D_qk=64 ≠ D_v=128 natively. diff_lambda (72 params total) routed to AdamW, excluded from Muon matrix groups.

hypothesis: Targeted AdamW β₂ ramp in last 10% of training only (step 90%→100%), from initial values (0.95–0.996) linearly to 0.999. exp_139 tried this over the full 65% warmdown (step 35%→100%) and got −0.002 within noise. Concentrating the increase in the final ~2700 steps (where LR is 5–15% of peak) is more targeted: at very low LR, gradient fluctuations dominate second-moment updates, making a slower EMA (higher β₂) most valuable. Clean rebase from exp_025.
notes: AdamW groups initial β₂: lm_head=0.96, embeddings=0.995, resid/x0/smear=0.95. All ramp to 0.999 over final 10% of training (~2700 steps at ratio=130). Muon schedule unchanged (warmdown=0.65, momentum 0.97→0.90, cosine WD). Only base_train.py modified. Rebased cleanly to exp_025.

hypothesis: Reduce Muon Polar Express ns_steps from 5→4 on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): with 4 iterations SVs converge to ~99% of unit vs 99.9% with 5, preserving more gradient SV structure so dominant directions get proportionally stronger updates. Opposite direction from failed exp_049 (5→7 was worse; 5→4 is unexplored).
notes: Single vendor edit: gpt.py setup_optimizer ns_steps=5→4 for all Muon param groups. No changes to optim.py polar_express_coeffs — uses first 4 of 5 pre-computed PE coefficients (valid subset; iteration stops early). Rebased cleanly to exp_025 to avoid accumulated stale state from prior discards (embedding_lr=0.4 etc.).

hypothesis: Add fullgraph=True to torch.compile(model) in base_train.py: forward is fully traceable (all conditionals are compile-time constants with dynamic=False), matching the existing compilation style of adamw_step_fused and muon_step_fused (both already use fullgraph=True). Eliminates any hidden graph breaks in the model forward, enabling better cross-op kernel fusion and consistent single-graph compilation of model + optimizer.
notes: The optimizer steps (adamw_step_fused, muon_step_fused) are already compiled with fullgraph=True in optim.py. The model forward is compiled without it. The GPT forward is fully traceable: kv_cache=None in training (specializes at compile time), window_pattern=L means all SDPA calls use is_causal=True path (no sliding-window fallback), loop over 12 blocks unrolled with compile-time-constant i, all conditionals (str(i) in self.value_embeds, i==backout_layer, x_backout is not None) are compile-time constants. fullgraph=True asserts zero breaks and enables better cross-layer fusion. No DDP wrapper on model (grad sync in DistMuonAdamW), so no DDP-hook graph-break risk.

hypothesis: Paragraph splitting for long documents before BOS-bestfit packing: documents >7000 chars (~2100 tokens at 3.3 ch/tok) are split at \\n\\n boundaries into semantically complete paragraphs (each ≥300 chars), each treated as an independent BOS-prefixed document. Recovers tail content from long documents otherwise discarded by best-fit cropping, while keeping splits at natural paragraph boundaries (not arbitrary byte-level cuts like exp_060/135).
notes: Rationale: at T=2048, ~35% of tokens are discarded as crop tails. Long documents (web articles, papers, etc.) are most affected — a 10K-char doc only contributes 2048 of its ~3000 tokens. Splitting at paragraph boundaries before packing lets the best-fit algorithm place all paragraphs as separate complete units (BOS+paragraph), recovering the lost tail content. Unlike exp_060 (arbitrary mid-doc byte cuts, scored 0.1985) and exp_135 (appended BOS at doc end, 0.2017), this creates semantically coherent training units. Only train split is affected; val loader unchanged. Thresholds (7000 chars, 300 char min para) target only truly long documents that would otherwise overflow T=2048.

hypothesis: Add V-norm (normalize value vectors to unit RMS per head after VE mixing, before attention): Q and K are already normalized; V-norm completes QKV normalization, decouples attention output scale from c_v init, and makes each head output ≈ unit-scale (attn weights sum to 1, each v is unit RMS). Zero extra params, negligible compute. Motivated by HybridNorm / QKV-norm literature (2025).
notes: Rebased to exp_025 (clean, no DIFF attention). Single change: added `v = norm(v)` in CausalSelfAttention.forward after value embedding mixing, before flash_attn call. Model structure, parameter count, and all other hyperparameters identical to exp_025. The norm function is the existing no-param F.rms_norm wrapper. Muon's normalized-gradient approach should handle the changed gradient scales gracefully.

hypothesis: Raise Muon momentum warmdown endpoint from 0.90→0.93 on clean exp_025 base (window=L, ratio=130): prior data — 0.85=-0.022, 0.90=best(0.2241), 0.97_const=-0.014 — shows a valley at 0.90. 0.93 (less reduction, 0.04 delta vs 0.07) is the only untested point in the less-penalized direction. Single-line code change in get_muon_momentum.
notes: Explored: endpoint 0.85 (exp_070, -0.022), 0.90 (exp_025, best), constant 0.97 (exp_062, -0.014). 0.93 is the midpoint toward the less-bad direction (0.97 side). Warmdown now decays 0.97→0.93 (delta 0.04) vs current 0.97→0.90 (delta 0.07). Expected to be within noise of best or slightly negative. Clean rebase from exp_025 removes exp_148's harmful AdamW beta2 ramp.

hypothesis: Set resid_lambdas weight_decay from 0.05→0.0 on clean exp_025 base: resid_lambdas initialize at 1.05-1.15 but AdamW WD decays toward 0, creating a bias below true optimum (at optimum the gradient≈0 so WD is uncontested). Removing WD lets them converge to their true optimal values.
notes: Rebased to clean exp_025 (reverts discarded ns_steps=4 from exp_149). Single change: resid_params AdamW weight_decay 0.05→0.0 in gpt.py setup_optimizer. resid_lambdas init at 1.15 (layer 0) to 1.05 (layer 11) via init_weights(). With wd=0.05 and scalar_lr*0.01=0.005, the WD force pulls toward 0 while the true optimum is ~1.1. At the true optimum the loss gradient is ≈0, so WD is uncontested and the equilibrium settles below the true optimum. Setting wd=0.0 removes this systematic bias.

hypothesis: Revert exp_151 paragraph splitting (hurt −0.028) and reduce best-fit buffer_size from 1000→100: smaller buffer means docs in each batch come from a narrower corpus window (~100K vs ~2M chars), creating more locally-coherent gradient signal. Observed pattern: buffer 1000→2000 gave −0.025 (exp_056), consistent with larger buffer = more temporal mixing = noisier gradients. Going below 1000 for the first time to test the opposite direction.
notes: Two changes: (1) remove _split_into_paragraphs() function and its call from refill_buffer — restores clean exp_025 data pipeline; (2) buffer_size default 1000→100 in tokenizing_distributed_data_loader_with_state_bos_bestfit. No experiment.py changes. Revert alone should recover ~0.224; buffer=100 is the new hypothesis. All other flags (ratio=130, seq=2048, DBS=32, window=L) unchanged.

hypothesis: Add learnable logit_bias (32768 params, zero-init) applied after lm_head before softcap, on clean exp_025 base (V-norm from exp_152 removed). Decouples unigram token prior from residual stream: model can learn to suppress rare/never-seen tokens without wasting a residual direction on base-rate distribution. Inspired by modded-nanogpt speedrun 'logit shift' technique. Training duration unchanged (excluded from scaling_params like VE).
notes: Two changes vs current workdir: (1) remove V-norm (exp_152 discard leftover, v=norm(v) was harmful), (2) add self.logit_bias nn.Parameter(zeros(padded_vocab_size)) — added to logits in float32 before softcap. AdamW with unembedding_lr, no weight decay (bias). Also updated num_scaling_params (scalars+=logit_bias.numel()), estimate_flops (nparams_exclude+=logit_bias.numel()), and setup_optimizer assert.

hypothesis: Rebase to clean exp_025 state: reverts exp_150 fullgraph=True regression (−0.021) and accumulated PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF fix from exp_142 (−0.017). Restores best-known sys recipe: BF16, ratio=130, window=L, default torch.compile mode.
notes: Sys domain is exhausted — all major precision (FP8 6+ attempts), compile modes (5+), FA3, weight averaging, gradient clipping, FP32 accumulation, memory allocator, cuDNN SDPA (budget overrun) have been tried and discarded. This rebase verifies the clean exp_025 baseline is recoverable and prevents further degradation from workdir state drift. No new sys-level changes remain with positive expected delta.

hypothesis: Couple WD decay to LR warmdown: hold WD constant at peak (weight_decay_scaled) during the constant-LR phase, then cosine-decay from peak→0 only during warmdown — instead of global cosine decay across all training (which wastes ~42% WD by warmdown_start at step 35%).
notes: The current global cosine WD schedule decays WD prematurely: by the time warmdown starts (at step ~35% of training = ~9500 of 27274 steps), WD has already fallen to ~58% of initial. Under the new schedule, WD stays at full value (0.28) during the constant-LR bulk training phase, then cosines from 0.28→0 over the warmdown window (same as LR going 1.0→final_lr_frac). This gives ~73% more regularization at warmdown_start. Motivation: WD and LR are both timescale parameters in AdamW (Lewkowycz et al., T_epoch framework); coupling them to decay together is theoretically principled. Distinct from: constant-WD (exp_057, -0.037 — WD never decays to 0) and global-cosine (current — decays too early). This is the 'LR-coupled' midpoint between those two extremes.

hypothesis: Schedule NorMuon beta2 from 0.9→0.95 linearly during warmdown (last 65% of training): keep fast variance adaptation (beta2=0.9) during warmup/constant phase, stabilise to 0.95 at end so decaying-LR steps are guided by stable second-moment estimates.
notes: Adds get_muon_beta2(it) schedule to base_train.py + updates Muon groups each step. Fixed beta2=0.9 is current baseline; exp_068 showed fixed 0.95 gives -0.003. A warmdown-only ramp avoids hurting the early/constant phase while gaining stability during the 17K-step warmdown where LR drops to 5% of peak. beta2 is already read dynamically from param_group by DistMuonAdamW._compute_muon each step — no optim.py changes needed.

hypothesis: Restore buffer_size from 100→1000: revert exp_155's harmful reduction (got 0.2013 vs 0.2241). The only structural diff from exp_025 in the current workdir; literature (arXiv:2511.18903) confirms data ordering changes are ineffective during LR warmdown, and at ratio=130 all 37% of epoch-2 data falls entirely in warmdown — explaining why all 20+ data experiments have been discards. Restoring the known-best data config to cleanly re-establish the exp_025 baseline for the data domain.
notes: buffer_size=100 (exp_155, 0.2013) → buffer_size=1000 (exp_025, 0.2241). Only change in dataloader.py is default buffer_size parameter. Experiment.py and all other vendor files are identical to exp_025 snapshot. Expected to land in 0.200–0.224 range (high variance at ratio=130) — this is a diagnostic revert to confirm clean baseline, not a novel improvement.

hypothesis: Add learnable per-feature output norm scale (ln_f_scale, 768 params, ones-init) applied after final RMSNorm before lm_head. Targeted variant of exp_072 (all-norms, −0.012): output norm is the only site where a learnable scale adds expressiveness without competing with attention QK dynamics or MLP c_fc gradients. Gives lm_head a direct 'feature importance' gradient path that AdamW on lm_head weights alone cannot provide as efficiently.
notes: ln_f_scale: ones-init (no-op at step 0), AdamW lr=0.02, wd=0.0. Excluded from scaling_params (doesn't change training duration). Training duration identical to exp_156. The motivation: RMSNorm normalizes to unit-RMS per feature, discarding per-feature magnitude. ln_f_scale recovers that signal. Unlike exp_072 (all norms), intermediate norms are left untouched to avoid disturbing QK-norm and MLP c_fc dynamics. The output norm is unique: it feeds lm_head directly with no subsequent weight matrix competition.

hypothesis: Add learnable per-layer attention temperature (qk_scale, 12 params, init=1.2) in CausalSelfAttention: replace hardcoded q*1.2/k*1.2 with learned per-layer scalar. Routed to AdamW (not Muon) at lr=0.01. Each layer learns its optimal attention sharpness; earlier layers may soften while later layers sharpen. exp_036 tried per-head per-layer (72 params, -0.024) — this is 6x more constrained and more stable.
notes: Rebased to exp_156 (logit_bias, no ln_f_scale). The only change is replacing hardcoded 1.2 with nn.Parameter([1.2]) per CausalSelfAttention layer. qk_scale excluded from transformer.h matrix group via ndim>=2 filter, put in dedicated AdamW group at scalar_lr*0.02=0.01. num_scaling_params and estimate_flops updated to exclude qk_scale. Zero budget impact (12 extra scalar params negligible vs 110M). No-op at step 0. | RuntimeError: Expected query, key, and value to have the same dtype, but got query.dtype: float key.dtype: float and value.dtype: c10::BFloat16 instead. (flash_attention.py:88 in _sdpa_attention)

hypothesis: Skip all intermediate val_bpb (eval-every=-1) and CORE evals (core-metric-every=99999, final still triggers via last_step) to reclaim ~20 min of eval overhead at ratio=130, redirecting that budget to 42% more training tokens (ratio 130→185). On exp_156 base (logit_bias). Estimated: 81.8 min pure training + 1 min final CORE ≈ 83 min total.
notes: Eval overhead at ratio=130: 13 CORE evals × ~1 min + 109 val_bpb evals × ~0.07 min ≈ 20 min. Removing these allows ratio=185 within the 90-min wall cap. core_metric_every=99999 > 0 ensures the last_step condition in base_train.py triggers the final CORE eval. val_bpb=None is handled gracefully by experiment.py. No architecture/optimizer/schedule changes — pure training efficiency reallocation.

hypothesis: Revert broken exp_158 WD schedule (hold-then-cosine, core=0.188) back to exp_025's global cosine WD, and probe --target-param-data-ratio=135 (midpoint between 130=keep/0.2241 and 140=-0.003); exp_121's ratio=133 result (0.2087) was likely dirty-workdir noise.
notes: Two changes vs current workdir: (1) revert get_weight_decay from exp_158's warmdown-coupled version back to global cosine (weight_decay_scaled * 0.5 * (1 + cos(pi*it/num_iterations))); (2) ratio 130→135 (+3.8% tokens, ~83 min). Clean WD + unexplored ratio midpoint. If ratio=133 (0.2087) was dirty workdir, the true optimum may sit between 130 and 140; ratio=135 is the cleanest unexplored probe.

hypothesis: Raise logit_bias LR from 0.008 (unembedding_lr) to 0.04 and beta2 from 0.96 to 0.99 on exp_156 base: logit_bias targets the near-stationary log-unigram prior, so 5x faster LR lets it converge early and free residual capacity for the conditional distribution; smoother beta2=0.99 gives well-calibrated steps throughout.
notes: Rebase to exp_156 (logit_bias arch change). Single opt change: logit_bias AdamW group lr 0.008->0.04, beta2 0.96->0.99. Theory: logit_bias has a stable optimal value (log-token-frequency). Faster LR means it reaches the optimal unigram prior correction earlier, allowing the residual stream to spend more capacity on conditional modeling. Higher beta2 gives a smoother/more accurate second-moment estimate suitable for a near-stationary parameter target. All other groups unchanged.

hypothesis: Increase BOS-bestfit buffer_size 1000→4000 on clean exp_156 base: 4× more candidates dramatically increases probability of finding a short doc to fill small remaining row slots, reducing the ~35% token crop rate. More complete documents seen per step improves training signal quality. CPU overhead stays well below GPU step time (~20ms vs ~170ms/step). Previous buffer tests (exp_155 buf=100, exp_160 buf=1000) were on a dirty baseline; this is the first clean test at 4000.
notes: Rebased to exp_156 (logit_bias arch, 0.2244). Single change: dataloader.py default buffer_size 1000→4000. Algorithm unchanged (BOS-bestfit). Memory cost ~32MB/rank. At ratio=130 (~27K steps, 32 rows/step), CPU overhead for best-fit scan ≈ 20ms/step vs 170ms GPU — not the bottleneck. Theory: prob(finding doc ≤ R tokens) scales with buffer_size, so 4× buffer → significantly fewer crop events → model sees ~5-10% more complete documents per training run.

hypothesis: Add learnable per-layer attention temperature (qk_scale, 12 params, init=1.2) in CausalSelfAttention: replace hardcoded q*1.2/k*1.2 with learned per-layer scalar cast to activation dtype. Routed to AdamW (not Muon) at lr=0.01. Each layer learns its optimal attention sharpness; earlier layers may soften while later layers sharpen. exp_036 tried per-head per-layer (72 params, -0.024) — this is 6x more constrained and more stable.
notes: Fixed dtype cast: qk_scale.to(q.dtype) before multiply. Master weights stay fp32 for optimizer precision, activations are bf16.

hypothesis: Skip all intermediate val_bpb (eval-every=-1) and CORE evals (core-metric-every=99999) to reclaim eval overhead, redirecting budget to 25% more training tokens (ratio 130→162). On exp_156 base (logit_bias). Calibrated from exp_163 overrun at ratio=185; ratio=162 estimated ~79 min total.
notes: exp_163 overran at ratio=185 (pure training ~85 min). Calibrating: ratio=162 → pure training 85×(162/185)=74.4 min + ~5 min overhead = ~79 min. core_metric_every=99999 still triggers at last_step. 25% more tokens than ratio=130 should yield meaningful CORE improvement along the still-beneficial scaling curve.

hypothesis: Apply C-AdamW cautious masking to adamw_step_fused: only update elements where exp_avg and grad agree in sign (mask = exp_avg * grad >= 0). Based on exp_156 (ratio=130, logit_bias). Prevents Adam from taking steps the current gradient contradicts; paper shows consistent LLM pretraining speedup.
notes: Cautious Optimizers (arxiv 2411.16085): C-AdamW adds a one-line mask to the Adam update — only apply where momentum direction agrees with current gradient. sign(update) == sign(exp_avg) since denom>0, so the mask is (exp_avg * grad >= 0). Applied to all AdamW groups (lm_head, embeddings, value_embeds, scalars, logit_bias). No renormalization — effective LR naturally reduces where sign disagreement is common. Muon's WD already uses a similar cautious-WD mask; this extends the idea to AdamW's main update. Rebased from exp_156 (reverting exp_165's discarded logit_bias LR change).

hypothesis: Combine warmdown-ratio 0.65→0.70 + AdamW β₂ warmdown schedule (initial→0.999 linearly during warmdown) on clean exp_025 base: both changes individually were closest-to-best discards (−0.0018 and −0.0022 respectively); the combination is untested and may be synergistic since longer warmdown gives β₂ ramp more steps to stabilize variance estimates at low LR.
notes: Clean rebase from exp_025. Changes: (1) experiment.py warmdown-ratio 0.65→0.70; (2) base_train.py adds get_adamw_beta2(it, initial_beta2) function + stores initial_beta2 per AdamW group after optimizer init + updates group['betas'] during warmdown. At warmdown=0.70 with ratio=130 (~27,274 steps), warmdown spans steps 8182→27274 (19,092 steps vs 17,728 at 0.65). AdamW β₂ ramps from group-specific initial values (embedding≈0.9, unembedding≈0.96, scalars≈0.95) to 0.999 over the same warmdown window. Muon momentum schedule unchanged (still 0.97→0.90 linear). WD schedule unchanged (global cosine to 0). arXiv:2508.01483 shows higher β₂ during cooldown improves performance; prior exp_139 tested this alone at 0.65 warmdown (−0.0022). This trial tests whether the combination with wider warmdown window is synergistic.

hypothesis: Skip val_bpb evals (eval-every=-1, saves ~7-8 min) and redirect budget to ratio 130→140 (+7.7% tokens) on clean exp_156 base. val_bpb is diagnostic only and doesn't affect training dynamics. CORE evals retained at core-metric-every=2000. Estimated wall ~84 min.
notes: Rebase to exp_156 (logit_bias, 0.2244). Only changes vs exp_156: (1) eval-every 250→-1 (skip intermediate val_bpb, saves ~7-8 min), (2) target-param-data-ratio 130→140. CORE evals kept at 2000-step cadence for quality signal. val_bpb purely diagnostic — skipping it does not affect weights/schedule. Budget calc: 60×(140/130)=64.6 min training + 14 CORE evals×1 min + 5 min overhead ≈ 83.6 min. exp_168 failed (0.2105) because it ALSO skipped CORE evals (core-metric-every=99999) and pushed to ratio=162. This experiment keeps CORE evals and uses a much smaller ratio increase.

hypothesis: Clip documents to row_capacity (T+1=2049 tokens) before buffering: docs longer than row_capacity can NEVER be placed whole in any row position (remaining space ≤ row_capacity always), so they accumulate as zombie buffer slots — only exiting when they happen to be the shortest doc in the buffer and get cropped to a tiny random prefix fragment. Pre-clipping converts these zombies into valid full-row-filler docs that are placed at pos=0 of a fresh row in clean complete BOS context (model sees first 2048 tokens of each long doc). Improves buffer diversity by eliminating dead slots; reduces pathological tiny-crop events. Rebased clean from exp_156.
notes: One-line code change in refill_buffer: `if len(tokens) > row_capacity: tokens = tokens[:row_capacity]`. Buffer_size stays at 1000 (exp_156 baseline). All other config unchanged (ratio=130, window=L, logit_bias arch). The zombie-doc problem is real but its magnitude depends on ClimbMix long-doc fraction — if few docs exceed 2049 tokens the effect will be small. This is the first experiment to address buffer slot utilization efficiency specifically.

hypothesis: Key offset in attention: second half of each head's key dims at position t ← position t-1's key values (after QK-norm+scale). Creates (current, prev-token) composite keys that architecturally enable induction-head patterns without any learned parameters. Zero-param change; causally valid; skipped for T=1 decode. Inspired by modded-nanogpt Record 58+ architecture.
notes: Rebased cleanly to exp_156 (logit_bias, no qk_scale). Single change: in CausalSelfAttention.forward, after q,k = norm(q,k) and scale by 1.2, apply k = torch.cat([k[:,:,:,:half], cat([k[:,:1,:,half:], k[:,:-1,:,half:]], dim=1)], dim=-1) when T>1. This replaces second-half key dims with prev-position's second-half keys. Each head's dot-product then mixes attention to 'current token at t' (first half dims) with 'previous token at t-1' (second half dims), directly supporting induction heads. No new parameters, no FLOPs change, no optimizer changes needed. Position 0 keeps original second-half keys (no predecessor).

hypothesis: Change final-lr-frac from 0.05→0.0 on clean exp_156 base: true linear decay-to-zero for the 65% warmdown phase, motivated by arXiv:2502.15938 showing linear D2Z consistently outperforms other schedules at compute-optimal training.
notes: arXiv:2502.15938 'Why linearly decaying the learning rate to zero works best' shows linear decay-to-zero outperforms at compute-optimal dataset sizes. Current schedule decays to 5% of peak LR (final_lr_frac=0.05); going to 0.0 gives slightly more aggressive cooling through the 65% warmdown. Single-variable change on rebased exp_156 (logit_bias arch, warmdown_ratio=0.65, linear shape unchanged). The LR at the actual last training step is ~0.00006×peak (not literally zero due to step indexing), so no numerical instability risk.

hypothesis: Raise lm_head and logit_bias AdamW beta2 from 0.96→0.98 on clean exp_156 base: lm_head receives cross-entropy gradients over 32K vocab, making its second moment noisy batch-to-batch; beta2=0.98 gives 50-step EMA window vs 25 steps at 0.96, stabilizing adaptive step sizes. Logit_bias shares the output pathway so gets the same change for consistency. Embeddings already use 0.995; lm_head at 0.98 bridges the gap.
notes: Single change: gpt.py setup_optimizer, lm_head betas (0.8,0.96)->(0.8,0.98) and logit_bias betas (0.8,0.96)->(0.8,0.98). All other groups unchanged. Rebased to exp_156 (clean baseline, no exp_169 C-AdamW masking). Motivation: lm_head/logit_bias are in the output logit computation path and have vocab-sized gradient tensors where batch-to-batch gradient variance is high. A wider EMA window reduces step-size variance. exp_165 changed logit_bias LR+beta2 together and discarded; this isolates beta2 only, with no LR change.

hypothesis: Replace relu^2 MLP with SwiGLU (8/3 expansion, same param+FLOP count): 3*(n_embd*(8/3)*n_embd) = 2*(n_embd*4*n_embd) = 8*n_embd^2. Also reverts discarded exp_173 key-offset code. Gating adds multiplicative capacity at zero extra cost.
notes: SwiGLU: hidden_dim = int(768*8/3)=2048 (already multiple of 64). c_fc ("up") + c_gate both init uniform ±0.4s; c_proj zero-init. Key offset from exp_173 removed (was -0.018). SwiGLU is the standard FFN in LLaMA/PaLM/Gemini at identical parameter count to relu^2 4x. Same FLOPs: 3*(2*d*(8d/3)*T) = 16*d^2*T == 2*(2*d*4d*T).

hypothesis: Mask loss at cross-document boundary positions (set target=-1, ignore_index=-1 in gpt.py): the last token of each packed doc no longer predicts the next BOS. This removes ~0.3% of tokens from training (the artificial "end-of-doc-A → BOS" transitions) to prevent the model from learning patterns that never appear at inference. Also reverts exp_172's harmful document clipping. Motivated by arxiv:2402.13991 (intra-document causal masking improves downstream quality).
notes: Two changes vs current workdir: (1) remove exp_172's doc clipping (if len(tokens) > row_capacity clip) — that experiment was discarded at 0.2016; (2) in the packing loop, track non-first BOS positions per row and after cpu_targets.copy_() set cpu_targets[row_idx, bos_pos-1] = -1 for each one. gpt.py forward() uses F.cross_entropy(..., ignore_index=-1) so these positions are correctly excluded from loss. ~6 positions masked per 2048-token row = 0.3% of training tokens. The rest is identical to exp_156.

hypothesis: Add mode="max-autotune-no-cudagraphs" to torch.compile: Inductor benchmarks GEMM/attention kernel implementations for our fixed shapes (768-dim, 2048-seq, 32 DBS) and selects the fastest, targeting 5-15% per-step speedup on GPU BF16. Restored ratio=130 and eval-every=250 to match exp_156 conditions exactly (ratio=140 trials consistently degrade quality due to logit_bias overfitting at longer horizons).
notes: Rebased on exp_156 (logit_bias, 0.2244). Only changes vs exp_156: (1) torch.compile mode → max-autotune-no-cudagraphs in base_train.py; (2) ratio restored to 130 (from exp_171's 140); (3) eval-every restored to 250 (from exp_171's -1). Tree analysis: exp_035 (ratio=140 from exp_025, no logit_bias) got 0.2208 (-0.003), while exp_171 (ratio=140 from exp_156, with logit_bias) got 0.2083 (-0.016). The logit_bias (zero-init, no weight-decay) overfits token unigram frequencies at longer horizons. max-autotune-no-cudagraphs is untested — could find better cuBLAS/Triton tilings for the 768×3072 and 768×32832 matmul shapes dominant in this model.

hypothesis: Add AdamW β₂ warmdown (initial→0.999 linearly during warmdown) on clean exp_156 base: at low LR, gradient noise dominates and higher β₂ smooths variance estimates. Stores initial_beta2 per group at optimizer creation, then ramps each AdamW group's β₂ toward 0.999 over the 65% warmdown phase. Also restores final-lr-frac 0.0→0.05 (revert exp_174). exp_139 tested this from exp_025 (−0.002, closest-to-threshold discard); testing from exp_156 with logit_bias group (β₂=0.96) which is new and may interact differently during convergence.
notes: Key changes vs exp_156: (1) base_train.py stores initial_beta2 per adamw group; (2) get_adamw_beta2() ramps β₂ from initial to 0.999 linearly during warmdown; (3) training loop updates group['betas'] each step for adamw groups; (4) experiment.py final-lr-frac restored to 0.05. Muon schedule, WD schedule, LR warmdown all unchanged. The logit_bias group (betas=(0.8, 0.96)) is unique to exp_156 and could benefit specifically from stabilized second moments as it converges to a near-stationary token-prior state during warmdown.

hypothesis: Remove cautious WD mask from muon_step_fused: switch from mask=(g*params>=0) gated WD to standard decoupled WD. Post-Polar Express, the orthogonalized gradient direction is less informative for sign-agreement tests than raw gradients, making unconditional WD cleaner and more principled.
notes: exp_169 tried C-AdamW (cautious masking on AdamW) and hurt -0.0145. This is the Muon side: standard WD removes the mask that gates on the post-orthogonalization gradient direction. After Polar Express, the g=US'V^T form has significantly transformed signs vs raw gradient, making the cautious criterion less meaningful. Standard WD (unconditional) provides uniform regularization. WD value unchanged at 0.28 (cosine-decayed per base_train.py). Rebased from exp_156.

hypothesis: Extend value embeddings (VE/ResFormer) from alternating 6 layers to all 12 layers: change has_ve() to always return True, so every attention block gets direct token-identity in the value stream (not just layers 1,3,5,7,9,11). Also reverts SwiGLU regression from exp_176 back to relu^2 MLP. VE params excluded from scaling_params so training token count unchanged (ratio=130 still drives iterations). Clean exp_156 base.
notes: All-VE motivation: even layers (0,2,4,6,8,10) currently lack direct token-identity access in their value computation — they must rely on context already embedded in the residual stream. Adding VE to these 6 layers lets the model skip the residual-stream bottleneck for token-identity information. Additional VE params (~150M bf16 = +300MB model weight per GPU, +1.2GB optimizer state per GPU) are manageable on 80GB the configured GPU environment. ve_gate (12→n_kv_head=6) correctly handles all 12 layers. relu^2 revert removes the -0.020 regression from exp_176 SwiGLU.

hypothesis: Revert exp_177's cross-document boundary loss masking: remove all_bos_positions tracking + cpu_targets masking loop from dataloader.py. exp_177 masked 0.3% of tokens at doc boundaries (set target=-1 before each non-first BOS) to avoid learning "last_tok_of_doc → BOS" patterns, but that experiment scored 0.2034 vs best 0.2244 (−0.021). Removing the masking restores pure BOS-bestfit packing on clean exp_156 base.
notes: The boundary masking in exp_177 was theoretically motivated (arxiv:2402.13991 on intra-document masking) but empirically harmful. The current workdir has this masking active; this trial reverts it. ignore_index=-1 in gpt.py is retained as a no-op (no -1 targets will be generated without the masking loop).

hypothesis: Revert max-autotune-no-cudagraphs compile regression (exp_178 added it, −0.021 vs exp_156). PyTorch 2.9.1 Inductor already auto-fuses BF16 cast+matmul (prologue fusion) and relu^2 epilogue by default — no manual config needed. The autotune mode added ~3 min kernel-benchmarking overhead and consistently regressed quality across exp_083/120/178 (each ~−0.020), likely by reducing effective training steps. Default torch.compile(dynamic=False) restores the proven exp_156 compile config.
notes: Single change vs exp_178: remove mode='max-autotune-no-cudagraphs' from torch.compile, back to torch.compile(model, dynamic=False). All other args (ratio=130, logit_bias in gpt.py, eval cadence) unchanged from exp_156 rebase. PyTorch 2.9.1+cu128 includes automatic prologue/epilogue fusion making explicit autotune config unnecessary and in practice harmful.

hypothesis: Increase --warmup-steps from 40→200 on clean exp_156 base: at ratio=130 (≈27,274 steps), 40-step warmup is only 0.15% of training — far shorter than the standard 0.5–2% recommendation. The Muon momentum warmup already runs 400 steps; aligning LR warmup to 200 steps reduces the mismatch and gives a more gradual LR ramp during early Muon optimization. No code changes, pure flag.
notes: Single flag change: --warmup-steps 40 → 200. All other args identical to exp_156 (ratio=130, warmdown=0.65, final-lr-frac=0.05). Rebased cleanly from exp_156 snapshot to remove exp_179's discarded beta2-warmdown code. Motivation: literature (Practical Efficiency of Muon, 2505.02222) uses linear warmup; 1-2% warmup is standard; at 27k steps the current 40-step warmup is unusually short.

hypothesis: Raise value_embeds AdamW LR from 0.5×→1.0× embedding_lr (0.15→0.30) on clean exp_156 base: value_embeds are ResFormer residuals of shape (vocab_size, kv_dim) — structurally identical to wte yet trained at half the embedding LR with no clear justification. At ratio=130 (~27K steps) this conservative haircut may leave value_embeds systematically under-adapted vs wte. Matching their LRs allows them to converge on equal footing. exp_084 tested the same change on exp_025 base (−0.0037, within noise floor ~0.005); on exp_156 base the dynamics with logit_bias may shift the outcome.
notes: Rebased cleanly to exp_156. Single change: gpt.py setup_optimizer, value_embeds_params lr=embedding_lr * dmodel_lr_scale * 0.5 → embedding_lr * dmodel_lr_scale. All other groups unchanged. On exp_025 base exp_084 gave 0.2204 (−0.0037 vs 0.2241 best). Testing whether the exp_156 logit_bias base changes the outcome.

hypothesis: Add post-norm (sandwich norm) to Block.forward: wrap each sub-layer output with norm() before residual add — norm(attn(norm(x))) and norm(mlp(norm(x))). Follows Gemma-2 / OLMo-2 design validated at scale. Zero-init c_proj means norm(0)=0 so init is unchanged. No new parameters (F.rms_norm is parameter-free). Stabilizes per-block gradient magnitude through residuals.
notes: Peri-LN / sandwich norm: apply RMSNorm on both input and output of each sub-layer (pre+post norm). Reference: arXiv:2502.02732 (Peri-LN paper), Gemma-2 tech report (arXiv:2408.00118), OLMo-2. Both Gemma 2 and OLMo employ this normalization. Our current nanochat only uses pre-norm. Change: Block.forward line 148-149, 2-line edit. No param change. Rebased cleanly to exp_156 (logit_bias, 0.2244) — reverts discarded exp_181 all-VE change.

hypothesis: Enable FP8 tensorwise training (--fp8) on exp_156 base, excluding lm_head from FP8 conversion to protect vocab gradient quality. exp_004 (ratio=12) hurt by -0.017, but ratio=130 is well-converged: gradients are stable and quantization noise is smaller relative to signal. relu^2 activation (vs SwiGLU) generates fewer outliers, reducing FP8 error. lm_head (768→32768) kept BF16 since its backward GEMM accumulates gradients over 32K vocab — most quality-critical matmul. All 72 attention/MLP linears use FP8 for ~30% step speedup.
notes: Rebased cleanly to exp_156 snapshot (94 files). Two changes: (1) experiment.py --fp8=True; (2) base_train.py fp8_module_filter adds `if 'lm_head' in fqn: return False`. relu^2 vs SwiGLU is lower-outlier; well-trained at ratio=130. Recent literature (ICLR 2025 'Scaling FP8 Training to Trillion-Token LLMs') confirms FP8 can match BF16 at scale. Even if neutral, opens door to ratio increase experiments.

hypothesis: Add document tail recycling: when a document is cropped to fill the remaining row space, prepend BOS to the tail (≥64 tokens) and add it back to the buffer as a BOS-aligned pseudo-document. Recovers ~35% of tokens discarded by crop events — these tail tokens were valid text but were thrown away. BOS prefix preserves the packing invariant (every placed segment starts with BOS). Recycled tails displace fresh parquet reads only marginally (~1 tail per row), preserving near-100% fresh-data diversity while boosting per-document completeness.
notes: Clean exp_156 base. Only change: 7-line addition in dataloader.py after the crop branch. `tail = doc[remaining:]` (Python list slice, no copy overhead); `[bos_token] + tail` reuses the existing tail list. Min tail length 64 tokens filters out sub-sentence fragments. Buffer dynamics: each crop-with-recycle replaces the consumed doc slot with the tail — net buffer size unchanged (refill threshold still 1000). The recycled pseudo-doc starts with BOS (token 0 of doc was originally BOS from `tokenizer.encode(prepend=bos_token)`; the tail starts at token `remaining`, which is mid-document content, but following BOS is consistent with how the model sees all packed documents). Risk: BOS semantics slightly diluted (~35% of BOS occurrences now prefix mid-doc fragments vs pure doc-starts). Upside: model sees complete document content instead of only the first `remaining` tokens of cropped docs.

hypothesis: Switch Muon weight-decay schedule from cosine-decay-to-zero to constant (weight_decay_scaled) on clean exp_156 base: the current schedule drops WD to 0 exactly at the final training step, leaving the model unregularized during late warmdown convergence; constant WD maintains L2 regularization throughout and may improve CORE-metric generalization.
notes: Single change: get_weight_decay() in base_train.py returns weight_decay_scaled (constant 0.28 for d12 at ratio=130) instead of weight_decay_scaled * 0.5 * (1 + cos(pi * it / num_iterations)). Current cosine schedule: WD starts at 0.28, decays to ~0.204 at warmdown start (step ~9546), reaches 0 at final step. New constant: WD = 0.28 throughout all ~27274 steps. All other args identical to exp_156 (ratio=130, warmdown=0.65, final-lr-frac=0.05, warmup-steps=40).

hypothesis: Raise Muon NorMuon variance-reduction beta2 from 0.9→0.95 on clean exp_156 base: the NorMuon paper (arXiv 2510.05491, cited in optim.py) recommends β2=0.95 for the factored second-moment EMA; the current 0.9 adapts too fast, producing a noisy per-neuron scale. Smoother variance estimate should stabilise the adaptive LR, especially during warmdown where gradients are already decaying.
notes: Single change vs exp_156: gpt.py setup_optimizer, Muon group beta2=0.9 → beta2=0.95. NorMuon paper (2510.05491) recommends β1=0.95, β2=0.95 as default configuration. Current code already uses momentum(β1)=0.95 for Muon but has β2=0.9 for the variance EMA. No other changes.

hypothesis: Differential Attention (arXiv:2410.05258, ICLR 2025): split Q,K,V along head_dim into halves (64-dim each), compute 4 SDPA calls with matching dims (flash backend), combine as y=(attn1-λ*attn2) where λ=sigmoid(diff_lambdas[i]) per layer. Paper shows ~35% token-efficiency gain. Depth-dependent init λ: 0.20→0.79 (matching paper's λ_init schedule). +12 scalar params. 4×attention kernel calls keeps flash backend; ~15% wall-time overhead estimated.
notes: Rebased cleanly to exp_156. Only changes: gpt.py Block.forward+CausalSelfAttention.forward implement diff attention (4 flash_attn calls with E=Ev=head_dim//2=64). GPT-level diff_lambdas parameter (12 scalars) routed to AdamW scalar group at scalar_lr*0.1. kv_cache inference path falls back to standard attention (not used for CORE metric eval which uses forward passes only). V split along head_dim so all SDPA calls have matching E=Ev=64 — avoids math-backend fallback that would occur with E=64,Ev=128.
