hypothesis: Reduce warmdown-ratio 0.65→0.50 and change LR warmdown from linear to sqrt shape on exp_156 base: sqrt decay (LR = final_lr_frac + (1-final_lr_frac)*sqrt(progress)) stays near peak LR longer during warmdown then drops sharply at the end, giving more effective stable-phase equivalent training; shorter warmdown-ratio also increases time at full LR. WSD dynamics research finds power-law decay shapes competitive; both changes push toward less aggressive early annealing.
notes: infrastructure sync failure

hypothesis: Reduce warmdown-ratio 0.65→0.50 and change LR warmdown from linear to sqrt shape on exp_156 base: sqrt decay (LR = final_lr_frac + (1-final_lr_frac)*sqrt(progress)) stays near peak LR longer during warmdown then drops sharply at the end, giving more effective stable-phase equivalent training; shorter warmdown-ratio also increases time at full LR. WSD dynamics research finds power-law decay shapes competitive; both changes push toward less aggressive early annealing.

hypothesis: Add per-head attention output gate (12×n_head Linear, AdamW) on clean exp_156 base: sigmoid gate conditioned on first 12 dims of pre-norm residual stream scales each head's output independently. Directly from modded-nanogpt PR #117 (−55 steps WR). Gate suppresses BOS attention-sink and learns head importance per token. 864 extra params, AdamW not Muon (asymmetric matrix).
notes: Gate applied in Block.forward: gate = sigmoid(attn_gate(x[..., :12])); attn_out reshaped to (B,T,n_head,head_dim), multiplied by gate, reshaped back. Init uniform(0, 0.02) → sigmoid≈0.5 at start, optimizer drives useful heads to 1.0. dim=12 empirically best in modded-nanogpt (beats dim=96, dim=768). See arXiv:2505.06708.

hypothesis: Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine warmdown (half-cosine from 1.0→final_lr_frac over warmdown_ratio=0.65 of training) on clean exp_156 base: cosine maintains ~15-20% higher effective LR through first 60% of the warmdown phase vs linear, then decays more steeply in the final 40%, giving the model more learning time at high LR before convergence. All other args unchanged (ratio=130, warmup=40, final-lr-frac=0.05).
notes: infrastructure sync failure

hypothesis: Reduce --eval-tokens 10× (80→8 ×524288) to reclaim ~6-10 min of val-bpb eval overhead, redirecting budget to ratio 130→155 (+19% training tokens) on clean exp_156 base. Eval reduction has no training-quality effect; 4M tokens gives SE<0.0001 on val_bpb. Log-linear scaling law (calibrated on ratio 100→130, +0.021) predicts +0.014 at 130→155.
notes: infrastructure sync failure

hypothesis: Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine warmdown (half-cosine from 1.0→final_lr_frac over warmdown_ratio=0.65) on exp_156 base: cosine maintains ~15% higher effective LR through first 60% of warmdown then decays more steeply at end, giving more training time near peak LR before convergence. Formula: final_lr_frac + (1-final_lr_frac)*0.5*(1+cos(π*(1-progress))). All other args unchanged (ratio=130, warmup=40, final-lr-frac=0.05). Retry of exp_195 which crashed due to internal sync infra failure, not code.

hypothesis: Reduce --eval-tokens 10× (80→8×524288, saves ~6-10 min) and redirect budget to ratio 130→155 (+19% tokens, ~32K steps): exp_196 had identical changes but crashed on infrastructure failure. Resubmit clean config.

hypothesis: Add stateless MuonEq-R/C pre-orthogonalization equilibration to muon_step_fused: for tall matrices normalize columns (polar express uses column Gram X^TX), for wide/square normalize rows (X X^T), before the Frobenius normalization + polar express. This makes the Gram matrix fed into the 5-iteration polynomial closer to identity, yielding a more accurate quasi-unitary output. Keeps post-polar-express NorMuon intact. arXiv:2603.28254 (MuonEq) shows pre-equilibration outperforms post-rescaling (NorMuon) on 130M/350M LLM pretraining; this adds it as a complementary step on top of NorMuon.
notes: infrastructure sync failure

hypothesis: Add stateless MuonEq-R/C pre-orthogonalization equilibration to muon_step_fused: for tall matrices normalize columns (polar express uses column Gram X^TX), for wide/square normalize rows (X X^T), before the Frobenius normalization + polar express. This makes the Gram matrix fed into the 5-iteration polynomial closer to identity, yielding a more accurate quasi-unitary output. Keeps post-polar-express NorMuon intact. arXiv:2603.28254 (MuonEq) shows pre-equilibration outperforms post-rescaling (NorMuon) on 130M/350M LLM pretraining; this adds it as a complementary step on top of NorMuon.
